contexts: Pre-war close — baseline
Day 3 — First trading session; S&P recovers from -1.2% intraday to flat
Day 4 — S&P -0.94%; Brent +4.71%; Hormuz closure formal
Day 5 — S&P bounces +0.78%; oil prices steady
Day 6 — Pentagon: $11.3B cumulative cost; S&P -0.56%
Day 7 — S&P -1.33%; WTI best week since 1983; worst S&P week in 5 months
Day 10 — Brent intraday $119.50; Trump says war "very complete"
Day 11 — Brent crashes 11.3% on false Hormuz escort report
Day 12 — IEA 400M bbl reserve release; CSIS: $16.5B cumulative
Day 13 — Brent closes above $100; S&P -1.52% (worst since Feb)
Day 14 — S&P hits 2026 low at 6,632; Brent $103.14; AUMF debate
Day 17 — Brent $106.18 on Fujairah/Dubai attacks; S&P futures +0.5%
Day 18 — Brent pulls back to $101; S&P recovers to 6,735; Ford fire contained
Day 19 — South Pars struck; Brent $108.43 (+52.7%); IRGC threatens Gulf energy; 3 officials killed
Day 20 — Iran retaliates on Gulf energy; Brent intraday $119.11; S&P 6,618 new 2026 low; Dow -768; Fed holds 3.50-3.75%
Day 21 — Quadruple Witching; S&P 6,506.48 new 2026 low; 3 consecutive weekly losses; Brent $112.19 (Fri close); Nasdaq -2.01%; Goldman 7,600 / JPMorgan 7,200

Day 24 — Trump postpones power plant strikes 5 days; Brent crashes 13.6% to $96.91 then recovers to ~$104; S&P surges ~1.7-2.2% to ~6,628; Dow +960; gold -6.3%; Asian markets did not benefit; defense/energy stocks fall
Day 25 — Oil rebounds 3-4% as Iran denies talks; S&P ~flat; defense -1.5%; energy outperforms; Pakistan emerges as mediator; Tripoli enters CENTCOM Friday

Day 26 — 15-point ceasefire plan; Brent -3.5% (largest drop of war) to $96.68; S&P +0.6% to ~6,596; all sectors green except energy; ARM +15%; gold rebounds +2.1% to $4,560; Iran rejects plan as 'maximalist'

Day 27 — Iran definitively rejects ceasefire; Israel kills IRGC Navy Cmdr Tangsiri; Brent surges to $105.85 (+3.8-6.4%); S&P ~6,550; gold drops to ~$4,429 (-2.4-2.9%); AP: 'stocks fall'; defense RTX +22.1% MTD; Carnival -27.4% MTD

Day 28 — Houthi missile barrage at Israel opens third front; WTI briefly crosses $100; Brent $112.57 (+4.22%); S&P 6,369 (-1.67%); Dow enters correction; VIX 31.05; fifth straight weekly decline; CNN 'extreme fear'; XOM new 52-wk high +39% YTD; gold $4,524

Day 31 — Mon open: S&P futures +0.7% on Powell 'well anchored' inflation remarks; Brent $114.90-$116+ intraday; WTI $101-103; Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran infrastructure; Islamabad summit outcome; defense stocks not sustaining surge despite combat; avg S&P member -17% from highs; avg Nasdaq member -31%

Day 32 — Iranian drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at Port of Dubai; Brent $112.78 settle nearing $120 on attack; gas crosses $4.00 nationally; 10Y extreme volatility 4.31-4.43%; Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure threatened — dual chokepoint; gold $4,578-4,600; Moody's recession prob 49%; XOM +28% YTD $171; CVX $210.71 near 52-wk high; DAL $64 UAL $91 down 15-20% monthly

Day 33 — Trump claims Iran asked for CEASEFIRE; markets surge on peace hopes; S&P 6,528.52 (+2.91% best day since May); Dow +2.49%; Nasdaq +3.83%; Brent drops 5% to ~$105 intraday; VIX collapses 17.5% to 25.25; Asian markets explode: Kospi +8.4%, Nikkei +5.2%; gold $4,746 (+1.9%); 10Y 4.31%; RTX $192.90 (+3.1%); LMT ~$610; airlines rally DAL $62.89 UAL $92.26; Apr 6 energy deadline 5 days out; Trump prime-time address 9 PM ET tonight; Rubio: 'we can see the finish line'

Day 34 — S&P 6,582.69 (+0.11%) — dramatic intraday reversal from post-Trump speech selloff on Iran-Oma transit protocol headlines; first weekly gain since conflict began (+3.4%); Dow 46,504.67 (-0.13%); Nasdaq 21,879.18 (+0.18%); VIX 23.87 (-2.73%); Brent $109.03 (+7.8%); WTI $111.54 (+11.4%, first close above $100 since Jul 2022); WTI-Brent spread inverted; gold $4,692.80 (-2.50%); 10Y 4.31% (-0.6 bps); XOM $160.69 (-5.2%); CVX $197.41 (-4.6%); DAL $58.78; UAL $91-95 (5% capacity cut); LMT $622.89 (+30% YTD); RTX $193-197; Apr 3 markets closed Good Friday; Apr 6 deadline 3 days; war approval 28% YouGov

Day 38 — S&P 6,611.83 (+0.44%) — four consecutive up sessions (+3.3% for week); Dow 46,669.88 (+0.36%); Nasdaq 21,996.34 (+0.54%); VIX 24.17 (+1.26%); Brent $111.25; WTI $111.81; gold $4,672-4,700; 10Y 4.335%; LMT $622.79 (+26% YTD); RTX $198.41 (+40% YoY); KTOS $74.09 (+10.07%); XOM ~$163.37 (+28-41% YTD); CVX $198.86 (+24% YTD); DAL $64-65; UAL ~$92; AAL ~$11 (-25-30% YTD); Easter Monday open (not a US holiday); 8 PM ET deadline tomorrow

Day 39 — S&P opened ~6,588 under pressure; range 6,579-6,618; down ~0.5-0.7% on Kharg Island strikes and escalating rhetoric; VIX 24.88 (+4.2%); WTI spiking toward $115 on Kharg strikes (50+ targets); Brent $109.87-111.68; gold $4,672-4,700; 10Y 4.323-4.353%; Goldman warns S&P could hit 5,400 on persistent disruptions; Kalshi 60% prob S&P below 5,900 in 2026; IRGC threatens strikes 'outside the region'; Vance: 'military goals achieved'

Day 41 — S&P 6,824.66 (+0.62%) — partial recovery after initial reversal; Brent settles ~$97.50; WTI ~$97.50; VIX ~19.4-21.0; Gold $4,743-4,782; 10Y 4.287-4.293%; Bloomberg: 'Stocks dropped and oil advanced as traders watched to see if the US-Iran ceasefire holds'; $12-18 Hormuz risk premium re-embedded; Soufan Center: ceasefire 'hovers on the verge of collapse'

Day 42 — S&P 6,816.89 (-0.11%) — best week since Nov (+3.6%); Dow +3.0% (positive for 2026); Nasdaq +4.7% (8-day winning streak); VIX 19.23 (down from 26 pre-ceasefire); Brent $96.66; WTI $95.63; dated Brent $131.97 ($35 premium over futures — physical scarcity); Gold $4,787 (+$1,568 YoY, 3rd straight weekly gain); 10Y 4.31%; March CPI: 3.3% YoY (+0.9% MoM, above 0.7% consensus) — gas +21.2% MoM (largest since 1967); core CPI 2.6% (below 2.7% consensus); UMich consumer sentiment 47.6 (all-time record low); real earnings -0.6%; LMT ~$614 (+30% YTD, PAC-3 $4.7B contract); RTX ~$202; XOM $152.51; CVX $188.55; DAL ~$64 (+12.5% Apr 8 earnings beat); UAL ~$91.35; airlines hike bag fees to $45; BoA cuts global growth 40bps to 3.1%; Fed holds 3.50-3.75%; markets price no rate cuts 2026

Day 45 — RISK-OFF MONDAY: S&P ~6,775-6,781 (-0.5% to -0.6%) — Islamabad collapse + Hormuz blockade trigger reversal; VIX 21.30 (+10.8%); Brent ~$103/bbl (+7.3%); WTI $97-105 wide intraday (+5-8%); Gold ~$4,724 (-0.6%); DXY 99.03 (+0.4%); 10Y 4.317% (+2 bps); Bitcoin ~$70,900 (-2.7% to -3.0%); Energy surges: XOM $157.61 overnight (+3.3%), CVX $192.39 (+2.0%); Defense mixed: LMT $607-$627, RTX ~$201; Airlines diverge: DAL $64.00 (+0.3%), UAL $91.35 (-1.3%); Goldman Sachs -4% premarket after FICC Q1 miss; Trump's Hormuz blockade effective 14:00 GMT today; IRGC declares any military vessel approaching Strait a ceasefire violation to be met with 'harsh and decisive' force; UK declines to join (Starmer: 'not getting dragged into the war'); markets now price only ~30% probability of a rate cut by December; Fed meets Apr 28-29

Day 46 — WAR LOSSES ERASED: S&P 6,967.38 (+1.18%, +81.14) erases all losses since Feb 28 baseline; Dow 48,535.99 (+0.66%, +317.74); Nasdaq 23,639.08 (+1.96%) — 10th straight up day; Russell 2000 2,705.67 (+1.32%); VIX 17.99 (-2.02%) — lowest since war began (peak 31.05 Mar 27); 10Y 4.248% (-5 bps from Apr 11); DXY ~98.13 near 1.25-month low; Gold rose ~2% to $4,800+ (highest since Mar 18); WTI $91.28 (-7.7%, -$7.59); Brent $94.79 (-4.2%, -$4.14); XOM ~$152-153; CVX $186.69; LMT $611.58 (off ATH $692 Mar 2); RTX ~$201.41 (record backlog); DAL ~$54.50; UAL ~$92.21 (+2% on merger news); AAL ~$14.11 (+7% on United-American merger proposal); Gas $4.118/gal (-$0.04); IEA April OMR confirms 10.1 mb/d March loss — largest disruption in history; oil plunges on peace-talk optimism (White House signals willingness to resume Islamabad talks; VP Vance: 'ball is in Iran's court'); API reports 8th consecutive weekly US crude inventory build (+6.1M bbl); IEA warns first annual oil demand decline since COVID; Q1 bank earnings beat estimates: Goldman Sachs Apr 13 (record equities trading $5.33B), JPMorgan $5.94 (beat $5.45), Citigroup $3.06 (beat $2.63), Wells Fargo $1.60 (stock -5%); March PPI well below expectations (+0.5% vs 1.1% est; core +0.1% vs 0.5% est); UMich consumer sentiment hits record low in April (below Great Recession and COVID); Fed holds 3.5-3.75%, markets price ~no rate cut at Apr 28-29 FOMC; Trump NY Post: talks 'could be happening over next two days'; IMF cut 2026 global growth forecast to 3.1%

Day 47 — S&P 500 BREACHES 7,000 FOR NEW ATH: S&P 500 new intraday ATH 7,003.71 (surpassing Jan 28 record of 7,002.28) — on track for first record close since January; Nasdaq +0.9-1.0% to ~23,852-23,875 — 10th consecutive positive session; Dow down ~48-97 pts (-0.1-0.2%); Russell 2000 flat to +0.33%; VIX 17.74-18.36 range (-3-5%); 10Y ~4.274%; Gold $4,830.31 (-0.24%) range $4,800-4,847; DXY 97.85 (-0.32%) 6-week low; Bitcoin $74,068-74,828 (8-day rally); WTI $90.90-93.73 mixed (Trading Economics $90.92 -0.40%; Investing.com $93.73); Brent ~$95-96.30 slightly higher (+0.2 to +1.6%); XOM $148.78 (-2.53%) war premium unwinding; CVX ~$186.54 (-2.73%); LMT $609-617 range AH $612.28 (-0.11%); RTX down 0.07%; BofA Q1 EPS $1.11, Rev $30.3B (+7% YoY), stock +2.5%; Morgan Stanley RECORD EPS $3.43, RECORD Rev $20.58B, net income +29% YoY, ROTCE 27.1%, stock +5%+; ASML beat Q1, raised FY guidance to $42.4-47.2B on AI demand; Morgan Stanley Wilson: 'the lows are in'; Fundstrat Tom Lee: markets pricing favorable Iran resolution; Iran IRGC Maj. Gen. Abdollahi THREATENS counter-blockade of Persian Gulf + Sea of Oman + Red Sea; Bloomberg: US and Iran considering 2-WEEK CEASEFIRE EXTENSION; Pakistan Army Chief Munir arrives Tehran with 'new message from Washington'; Trump: war 'very close to over'; Iran's blockade response 'pretty amazing'; Treasury Sec Bessent: 'optimistic' about $3 gas by June-September; Hezbollah launches ~30 rockets at northern Israel; Senate War Powers vote at 2 PM ET (expected fail); gas $4.108/gal (-1 cent)

Day 48 — S&P 500 RECORD CLOSE 7,041.28 / SECOND STRAIGHT DAY ABOVE 7,000 — PEACE PREMIUM PRICED: Apr 16 closes: S&P 500 RECORD CLOSE 7,041.28 (+0.26%); Nasdaq 24,102.70 (+0.36%) — 12-day win streak, longest since July 2009; Dow 48,578.72 (+0.24%); Russell 2000 2,719.60; VIX ~18.1 (down sharply from Mar 27 war-peak close of 31.05); Week-to-date through Apr 16: S&P +3.3%, Nasdaq +5.2%; Brent closed ~$94.13/bbl; WTI ~$93; ExxonMobil and Chevron fell ~5% on war premium compression (XOM +27.6% YTD); Gold $4,818/oz (+~48% YTD); 10Y UST 4.311%; 2Y 3.778%; 30Y 4.937%; Iranian rial collapsed to ~1,544,000 IRR/USD (-42% YoY); March CPI +3.3% YoY (highest since May 2024) / +0.9% MoM (largest monthly jump since June 2022); core 2.6%; UMich preliminary April consumer sentiment 47.6 — ALL-TIME LOW in 74-year series, below June 2022 reading of 50, down 10.7% from March; 1-year inflation expectations 4.8% (biggest monthly jump since Apr 2025); Fed funds 3.50-3.75%; Chicago Fed Goolsbee: cuts may slip to 2027 if oil stays elevated; EIA crude inventories -9.13M bbl vs +154K expected; Lebanon 10-day ceasefire effective 5:00 PM ET Apr 16 — second diplomatic front opens alongside Iran ceasefire; House rejected H.Con.Res.38 War Powers Resolution 213-214 Apr 16 — Massie lone GOP yes, Golden lone Dem no, Davidson present; 60-day WPR deadline ~May 1; ceasefire on Iran front ~240 hrs (expires ~Apr 21-22); Pakistan Army Chief Munir + Naqvi in Tehran Apr 15-16 — Iran agreed 'in principle' to third-party nuclear monitoring (4 countries + IAEA); enrichment-moratorium gap: US 20 yrs vs Iran ~5, 10-yr compromise on table; Trump Apr 17 declared US-Iran deal 'very close,' will travel to Islamabad for signing; Pope Leo XIV from Cameroon Apr 16: 'masters of war pretend not to know...' — Trump retaliated calling Pope 'weak on crime'; Vance (Catholic convert) told Pope to 'stay out of politics'; Morgan Stanley Wilson: 'the lows are in'

Day 49 — S&P 500 / NASDAQ RECORD CLOSES — WAR-PREMIUM UNWIND: Apr 17 closes: S&P 500 RECORD 7,126.06 (+1.20%, +84.78); Dow 49,447.43 (+1.79%, +868.71); Nasdaq RECORD 24,468.48 (+1.52%, +365.78); Russell 2000 2,776.90 (+2.11%); VIX 17.48 (-2.56%); Week: S&P +3.6%; 10Y 4.24% (-6.8 bps); DXY 97.73 (-0.26%); Gold futures $4,879.60/oz (+1.48%); Silver $81.97 (+4.14%); Bitcoin ~$76,038-78,000 (+0.55 to +4.5%). Oil collapsed in sharpest single-session move since Apr 8 on Iran morning claim Hormuz was 'completely open' — Brent $90.38 settle (-9.0%, intraday low ~$86.84); WTI $83.85 settle (-12.0%, intraday low ~$79.78) — five-week lows per Trading Economics. Defense rebounded: ITA $233.68 (+2.03%), 1-yr TR +55.93%. Energy (XLE) extended sharp losses — Benzinga est XLE constituents face ~$76B annualized revenue hit at current crude prices. Airlines ripped higher: ALK +13.6% (top R1000 gainer), DAL $71.72 (+2.6%) with implied ~$1.5B Q2 pretax fuel tailwind; JETS up double digits WoW. Dated Brent spot all-time high >$144 vs futures ~$109 (~$35 premium) per Al Jazeera. Only ~17 vessels transited Hormuz on reference Saturday Apr 11 vs ~130/day pre-war; first VLCC cleared Apr 14. AAA gas national avg $4.076/gal (-1.7 cents DoD, +90.9 cents YoY). March CPI 3.3% YoY; gas component +21.2% MoM. UMich consumer sentiment 47.6 — all-time low. FactSet: 10% of S&P reported, 88% beating, aggregate 10.8% above consensus; fwd P/E 20.9. IMF cut 2026 global growth forecast, warned further escalation 'a close call for global recession.' Fed Funds futures repriced aggressively toward December 2026 cut (prior: on-hold into 2027). Treasury extended Russian seaborne-oil sanctions waiver through May 16. Polymarket implies 78.5% probability Hormuz traffic does NOT normalize by April 30 (only ~21.5% normalization odds). War-risk insurance mid-April 0.8-2.5% of hull value (8-20x pre-war). Markets priced ceasefire on Friday; diplomacy did not deliver by Saturday — dangerous Monday gap risk.

Day 52 — TOUSKA MONDAY — CEASEFIRE-CLOCK RE-PRICED: Apr 20 intraday: S&P 500 ~7,119 (-0.1 to -0.2%) after opening sharply lower; Dow ~flat; Nasdaq -0.2 to -0.4% (24,395-24,420 range); VIX spiked >21 early then settled ~19.98 (+14%); oil surged 5-7% on USS Spruance seizing M/V Touska (Apr 19) and Iran parliament's draft 'Tehran Tollbooth' law — Brent ~$95.42 (+5.58-7%); WTI ~$87.93 (+~6%); Brent-WTI spread ~$15 (vs normal $6) reflects Hormuz disruption; 10Y 4.26-4.28% (+2 bps on Hormuz fears); Gold $4,806-$4,879 range (4th consecutive weekly gain); DXY ~97.90-98.10; Bitcoin ~$74,000-76,000. Energy bid: XOM/CVX reversing last week's war-premium compression; XLE up on crude spike. Airlines reversed Friday's ALK/DAL rally: JETS ~$26.63 Apr 17, softening Apr 20 as crude surge ate fuel tailwind. Defense mixed with RTX reporting Apr 21 ($268B backlog, 850+ Tomahawks fired); LMT +~40% YTD (THAAD contract 4x to 400/yr); iShares Defense Industrials Active ETF -12.4% last month on short-war expectations. Iran FM Baghaei says Tehran 'has no plans' for Round 2 Islamabad talks, calls US blockade 'war crime and crime against humanity'; Pakistan says Iran 'shown openness' — DISCREPANCY. Vance confirmed joining Witkoff and Kushner; US C-17 landed Nur Khan; Islamabad Red Zone locked down. Trump Apr 17-20 threats: 'knock out every single Power Plant, and every single Bridge, in Iran'; 'blowing everything up and taking over the oil'; peace deal would involve US taking control of Iran's nuclear material. Hormuz throughput >90% below pre-war baseline; ~10 mb/d disrupted. Ceasefire expires ~20:00 ET Tuesday Apr 21 — binary event Apr 20-21 Islamabad. Aptus Capital's David Wagner on CNBC: 'The war with Iran is now in the rearview mirror for the market' — the 'TACO trade' (Trump Always Chickens Out) priced even as Hormuz signals escalation. War-risk premiums 0.5-1.0% per transit; Lloyd's JWC expanded high-risk zone to entire Persian Gulf. University of Michigan April sentiment 47.6 — 74-year record low; 1Y inflation expectations 4.8%; March CPI +0.9% MoM (largest since June 2022). FedWatch pricing rising probability of December cut.

Day 53 — DIPLOMATIC KNIFE-EDGE / CEASEFIRE EXPIRY PUSHED TO WED: Apr 21 close: S&P 500 7,064.01 (-0.63%); Dow 49,149.38 (-0.59%); Nasdaq 24,259.96 (-0.59%); Russell 2000 ~2,782 (off Apr 20 record 2,792.96); VIX 19.50 (+3.34%); Brent ~$98.20 close (ahead of Apr 22 Hormuz seizures); WTI $89.22 (-0.50%); 10Y UST 4.258%; DXY ~97.85-98.07; Gold futures $4,760.20 (+0.86%); Bitcoin ~$75,900-$76,650; JETS $29.01 (+2.55%, 52-wk high $29.23); XLE ~$55.07 (off 52-wk high $63.46 Mar 30); ITA ~$231.60-$231.85 (stockanalysis.com showed -3.42% 1-day conflicting with other sources — FLAG). Q1 EARNINGS BEATS (April 21): Northrop Grumman, RTX, Lockheed Martin, UnitedHealth (+6% pre-market), GE Aerospace, Danaher, 3M, Halliburton; United Airlines after close. Alaska Air (ALK) pulled full-year profit forecast citing war jet-fuel costs. JPMorgan's Dubravko Lakos-Bujas raised S&P 500 year-end target 7,200 → 7,600 — institutional conviction that conflict is receding. Trump on CNBC Apr 21 morning: did NOT expect to extend ceasefire; reversed by ~16:22 ET with Truth Social indefinite-extension post. AAA national gas avg $4.020/gal (-$0.002 d/d). US delegation (VP Vance, Witkoff, Kushner) departed Washington morning Apr 21 — Air Force Two reached Joint Base Andrews before trip officially cancelled after Iran declined to attend; Witkoff and Kushner remained in DC for consultations. Iran FM Araghchi Apr 21 called US blockade and Touska seizure 'an act of war and a material breach of the ceasefire'; UN Amb. Iravani filed formal protest to SecGen Guterres. CENTCOM tally: 27-28 vessels turned back since Apr 13.

Day 54 — RECORD CLOSES DESPITE HORMUZ ESCALATION: Apr 22 close: S&P 500 ALL-TIME RECORD 7,137.90 (+1.05%, +73.89); Nasdaq Composite ALL-TIME RECORD 24,657.57 (+1.64%); Dow 49,490.03 (+0.69%, +340.65); VIX 19.42 (+2.64% overnight); Brent $99.03 (+0.5%) briefly piercing $100/bbl on IRGC seizures of MSC Francesca and Epaminondas; WTI $90.86 (+1.3%); Gold ~$4,750. Markets rebounded on Trump's ~16:22 ET Apr 21 Truth Social post EXTENDING Iran ceasefire INDEFINITELY (pending Iranian 'unified proposal'), while oil lifted on fresh IRGC maritime aggression. Islamabad Round 2 collapsed: Vance trip cancelled after Iran declined; Witkoff + Kushner stayed in Washington; unresolved — enriched uranium stockpile, Hormuz status, blockade, Iranian war-reparations demand. Pakistan Info Min. Tarar noted Iran's formal attendance response 'is still awaited' before cancellation. AAA national gas avg $4.022/gal; California highest $5.84, Oklahoma lowest $3.38. FY2027 DoD budget request Apr 21: $1.5T (+42%), including $75B drones, $66B shipbuilding, $102B fighters/bombers. Pentagon (closed-door brief to Congress): first six days of war cost $11.3B with $5.6B munitions burn in first two days. CSIS: $16.5B by Day 12. AEI: $35B+ by Apr 1. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct / up to $210B total. Harvard's Linda Bilmes (Apr 15): $2B/day, >$1T long-run including veteran care. UNDP: $120-194B regional GDP damage. Polymarket implied Hormuz-normalization-by-Apr-30 probability ~21.5% (unchanged from Apr 17).

Day 55 — PULLBACK FROM RECORD HIGHS / BRENT BACK OVER $100 / 'SHOOT-AND-KILL' ROE: Apr 23 intraday (closing values not finalized): S&P 500 ~7,109 (-0.4%); Dow ~-238 pts (-0.5%); Nasdaq ~-0.3%; Tesla -2.2% after disappointing capex guidance; IBM and ServiceNow dropped >7% and >12% respectively after-hours Apr 22; CSX +5.8% (earnings beat). Brent back above $100/bbl at ~$103.38-$103.67/bbl on Trump's 'shoot-and-kill' order for Hormuz mine-laying boats; WTI ~$94.46 (+1.67%). Gold $4,735.60 (-0.37% overnight); Bitcoin $78,044.50. Defense stocks pulled back as markets priced truce: LMT $554.08 (-3.12%), NOC $585.20 (-4.24%), RTX $180.56 (-3.53%), GD $318.46 (-2.17%), LHX $331.82 (-2.49%), LDOS $150.38 (-1.84%); BA $229.94 (+4.92%) on earnings beat. Airlines pressured: United Airlines gave disappointing guidance citing rising fuel costs; Alaska Airlines expects 2026 fuel costs to jump up to 81%, had Q1 loss of $193M; Lufthansa removing 20,000 short-haul flights through October (~40,000 metric tons jet fuel saving); Air Transat adjusting 2026 schedule. Semiconductor index (^SOX) on 17-day winning streak pre-Day 55; chip stocks added >$3T market cap since Mar 30. AAA national gas avg $4.031/gal (+$0.009 d/d). Trump ordered Navy to 'shoot and kill any boat' laying Hormuz mines (Truth Social) — most significant ROE change since Apr 13 blockade. CENTCOM blockade tally rose to 31 vessels turned back. US Senate defeated Baldwin war-powers resolution 55-46 (5th failed attempt). Pentagon boarded another 'sanctioned stateless vessel' carrying Iranian oil overnight; Reuters reports ≥3 Iranian-flagged tankers intercepted in Asian waters. Lebanon-Israel Round 2 talks convene in Washington (White House). IRGC Deputy Speaker Haji Babaei announces first Hormuz toll revenue deposited in Iran's Central Bank account. Citi analyst: oil could rise to $110 if Hormuz remains disrupted another month; global inventories could hit 8-year lows by end of June even if war ends immediately. UMich final April consumer sentiment due Apr 24 (prior preliminary: 47.6 all-time low).

Day 56 — RECORD HIGHS, OIL FALLS ON PEACE-TALK HOPES: Apr 24 close: S&P 500 ALL-TIME RECORD 7,165.08 (+0.80%, +56.68); Nasdaq Composite ALL-TIME RECORD 24,836.60 (+1.63%, +398.09); Dow 49,230.71 (-0.16%, -79.61); Russell 2000 2,787.00 (+0.43%); VIX 18.71 (-3.11%); Gold $4,725.40/oz (+0.03%); BTC/USD $77,615.66 (-0.35%). Brent settled $105.33 (-on day; intraday touched >$106 on Asia open before easing); WTI $94.40 (-$0.06). Weekly: Brent ~+14%, WTI ~+17% — among largest weekly energy moves in months. Notable movers: INTC +23.60% (best day since 1987 post-Q1 earnings beat); NVDA retook $5T market cap; CMCSA -8% (Deutsche Bank downgrade); CHTR -23% (subscriber losses); ServiceNow (NOW) Apr 23 -18% on Middle-East-impacted subscription growth. Defense sector still elevated YTD: ITA (iShares US Aerospace & Defense) +66% trailing 12M / +8% YTD; RTX YTD +7.11% (Q1 revenue $24.24B +12.1% YoY, $268B backlog); LMT YTD +20.79% (Q4 2025 revenue $20.32B +9.1% YoY, record $194B backlog, 191 F-35s delivered in 2025 vs 110 in 2024). Airlines remain under pressure (American Airlines / major carriers; T&E analysis: long-haul EU flights +$104/passenger fuel cost; Qatar/Emirates reducing frequencies). KEY DRIVERS Apr 24: (1) Iranian FM Araghchi flew to Islamabad evening Apr 24; WH announced Witkoff/Kushner depart Apr 25 for direct US-Iran talks; markets priced higher probability of de-escalation; (2) Israel-Lebanon ceasefire EXTENDED 3 weeks (Trump-brokered Apr 23); (3) UMich final April Consumer Sentiment 49.8 (above 47.6 prelim, above 48.6 DJ consensus, but still LOWEST ON RECORD — below COVID, 2008 crisis, post-Ukraine 2022 peak inflation); (4) CENTCOM publicly announced 3 U.S. carriers in Middle East simultaneously for first time since OIF 2003; (5) NYT investigation said U.S. has expended >1,200 Patriot interceptors, ~1,100 long-range stealth cruise missiles, >1,000 Tomahawks (~10x annual procurement) during Epic Fury — WH Press Sec. Leavitt called premise 'false'; (6) AAA national gas avg $4.022/gal (-$0.009 d/d); (7) UN warned 30M+ people pushed into poverty even if war ends tomorrow; (8) Trump extended Jones Act waiver 90 days (allows non-US flagged vessels to deliver oil/LNG to US ports). 60-day War Powers deadline ~May 1 (~6 days). Critical inflection: Apr 25-27 weekend Witkoff/Kushner-Araghchi diplomacy.

Day 59 — S&P + NASDAQ FRESH RECORD CLOSES DESPITE STALLED IRAN DIPLOMACY / OIL UP / GAS DOWN: Apr 27 close: S&P 500 NEW ALL-TIME RECORD CLOSE 7,173.91 (+0.12%, +8.83) — eighth record close in past two weeks; Nasdaq Composite NEW ALL-TIME RECORD CLOSE 24,887.10 (+0.20%, +50.50); Dow 49,167.79 (-0.13%, -62.92) DIVERGED — weighed by rate-sensitive blue chips ahead of Fed Apr 28-29 meeting; Russell 2000 2,788.19 (+0.04%, +1.19); VIX 18.02 (-3.69%, -0.69) — vol compression continues; 10Y UST 4.34% (+3 bps); Gold $4,697.70 (-0.91%); BTC/USD $76,853.98 (-2.02%). WTI June futures (CLM26) $96.68 (+$2.28 / +2.42%) on Iran-proposal/talks-stall rebound, reversing Friday's drop on Witkoff/Kushner-trip optimism. Brent FLAGGED — official settlement not cleanly sourced; intraday $106.00–$108.00 band, eased back toward $106 in afternoon trading on Iran-proposal headlines (Trading Economics); Fortune syndication reported $106.73 at 9 a.m. ET. Goldman Sachs (Apr 27 client note) raised Q4 2026 Brent forecast to $90/bbl (from $80) and WTI to $83/bbl (from $75) citing 14.5 mb/d Persian Gulf crude production losses driving global inventories to draw at 'record 11–12 mb/d pace in April.' KEY MOVERS: Micron +6%; Nvidia new ATH; AI/chip optimism (Magnificent Seven reporting this week) outweighed stalled Iran diplomacy. AAA national gas avg $4.031/gal (essentially flat from $4.022 Apr 24; AAA Apr 23 weekly bulletin reported $4.03) — down from Apr 9 peak of $4.16, but ~35% above pre-war. AVIATION-SECTOR FINANCIAL STRESS CRYSTALLIZING: Spirit Airlines bailout request and Frontier/Avelo joint $2.5B jet-fuel-cost ask cited Apr 27 — not a flight-cancellation count but a sector-stress signal. KEY DRIVERS Apr 27: (1) Iran transmitted written proposal via Pakistani mediators offering Hormuz reopening + ceasefire extension in exchange for U.S. lifting naval blockade — explicitly DEFERRING nuclear-program negotiations (Axios primary; Bloomberg, AP, CNBC, Al Jazeera confirm); Trump told reporters proposal 'much better' but Iran 'offered a lot but not enough'; WH convened Situation Room meeting; no public readout EOD. (2) IRGC Telegram statement countered: control of Hormuz is 'definitive strategy' — factional split within Tehran leadership now overtly visible. (3) Iran FM Araghchi met Putin and Lavrov in St Petersburg — Russia entering diplomatic frame. (4) European posture: EC President Von der Leyen 'too early' to lift Iran sanctions; German Chancellor Merz says Iran is 'humiliating' the U.S. (5) Lebanon ceasefire deadliest day since truce — 14 killed Apr 26 (Lebanese MoPH cumulative 2,521 / 7,750); 1 IDF KIA, 6 wounded. (6) AP HEADLINE: 'U.S. says it's clearing Iranian mines in latest push to open the Strait of Hormuz.' (7) Sanctioned Mordashov-linked superyacht NORD transited Hormuz Saturday (Bloomberg shipping data). (8) Microsoft-OpenAI exclusivity ending — Magnificent Seven headline that briefly competed with war news. War Powers May 1 60-day deadline T-4 days; no Apr 27 floor action; last (5th) Senate WPR vote Apr 22 (Baldwin) failed 46-51; Sen. Susan Collins indicated may join Democrats post-deadline. Aptus 'TACO trade' framing intact: AI/chip earnings cycle + Fed expectations of unchanged rates dominate; war risk being treated as background despite (a) talks visibly stalled, (b) oil +2.4%, (c) Lebanon deadliest post-truce day, (d) Goldman raised Brent forecast $10/bbl.

Day 60 — RECORD-CLOSE PULLBACK ON OPENAI/AI CAPEX FEARS / BRENT BREAKS $111: Apr 28 intraday (Apr 27 settlements as reference): S&P 500 ~-0.7% from Apr 27 record 7,173.91; Nasdaq Composite -1.3% (semis weak — VanEck SMH ETF -3% intraday on WSJ OpenAI revenue/AI capex concerns; Oracle -7.5% premarket; Nvidia -3%); Dow +0.2% (defensives bid); Russell 2000 +0.4%; VIX 18.02 (compressed). Brent crude broke above $111/bbl for the first time since late March on Trump's reported dissatisfaction with Iran's Hormuz-only proposal (NYT) — intraday $110.85 (high $111.57, +3.9%); WTI crossed $99/bbl (intraday $99.40, high $99.75, +2.8%). Goldman Sachs Q4 2026 forecast raised Apr 27: Brent $90 / WTI $83 (from $80/$75). KEY MOVERS Apr 28: GE Vernova +7% on Q1 beat; GM +4% (raised 2026 guide); Nucor +3.8%; Boeing +3% (smaller-than-expected loss); UPS -3%; Oracle -7.5% premarket; Nvidia -3%. Defense (RTX/LMT) broadly flat post-Patriot/Tomahawk replenishment cycle; oil majors (BP/CVX/XOM) bid on Brent surge. Equity weakness Apr 28 driven primarily by WSJ OpenAI revenue/AI capex concerns, NOT war news — oil-driven cyclical inflation worries secondary. Fed FOMC decision due Wed Apr 29 (CME futures pricing 100% hold). AAA national gas avg $4.022/gal (-$0.009 d/d from $4.031 Apr 27); California highest $5.83, Oklahoma lowest $3.38. KEY DRIVERS Apr 28: (1) Trump national-security team REVIEWED Iran's Pakistan-mediated proposal (lift U.S. blockade for revised Hormuz transit framework, nuclear deferred) and concluded he 'doesn't love the proposal' — Sec. Rubio (Fox): Iran wants to 'normalize a system in which the Iranians decide who gets to use an international waterway' — unacceptable; Trump publicly: 'We have all the cards. If they want to talk, they can call us.' (2) Araghchi met Putin and Lavrov in St Petersburg Apr 27; Putin: 'we see how courageously the Iranian people are fighting for their independence'; received written message from Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. (3) IDF expanded strikes into Bekaa Valley Apr 27 (>20 Hezbollah-linked sites; first such expansion since Apr 16 Lebanon ceasefire). (4) Houthi Red Sea track REACTIVATED — MV Andromeda Star (Russian-crude tanker) struck by 3 ASBMs ~15 nm SW of Mokha Apr 26. (5) Iranian army spokesperson Apr 28: Iran 'still in a war situation.' (6) Qatar at GCC summit warned against weaponizing Hormuz, cited 'frozen conflict' risk; UN SecGen Guterres warned of global food emergency. (7) Sen. Tillis/Curtis defection watch on War Powers Resolution; May 1 60-day deadline T-3. War Powers May 1 statutory deadline operationally non-binding — no scheduled floor vote on Massie/Khanna H.Con.Res.38 (94 cosponsors) or Kaine companion in window. UMich final April sentiment 49.8 (record low); CME FedWatch 100% hold Apr 29. 'TACO trade' (markets dismiss war risk pending escalation) intact through Apr 28.

Day 61 — TRUMP REJECTS IRAN PROPOSAL VIA AXIOS / PENTAGON $25B WAR-COST DISCLOSURE / BRENT TOPS $118 INTRADAY / UAE QUITS OPEC: Apr 29 intraday: S&P 500 near flat to -0.2% from Apr 28 close 7,138.80; Nasdaq -0.2%; Dow ~flat; final closing prints not confirmed at compile time. Brent crude topped $118/bbl intraday — peaked $118.33 at 12:10 ET (+6.0% from Apr 28 settlement $111.26 — highest since June 2022, eight straight session gains); WTI peaked $106.37 (+6.4%, third straight session gain); Brent eased to ~$116.53 by late Apr 29 (Trading Economics, +4.74% on day). KEY DRIVERS Apr 29: (1) Trump in AXIOS PHONE INTERVIEW explicitly REJECTED Iran's Pakistan-mediated 'Hormuz-for-blockade-lift' proposal — 'They want to settle. They don't want me to keep the blockade. I don't want to [lift it], because I don't want them to have a nuclear weapon... [the blockade is] somewhat more effective than the bombing... They are choking like a stuffed pig'; says Iran's oil storage and pipelines 'are getting close to exploding.' (2) Trump Truth Social early morning: 'Iran can't get their act together... No more Mr. Nice Guy!' (3) HASC HEARING — first congressional testimony by SecDef Pete Hegseth since war began. Acting Pentagon Comptroller Jules Hurst pegged cumulative Operation Epic Fury cost at ~$25 BILLION (mostly munitions); Hegseth said any Iran-specific supplemental request will be 'less than $25B'; Hegseth identified 14 critical munitions for replenishment (Patriot, THAAD, SM-3, SM-6, AMRAAM, JASSM, PrSM); FY2027 Pentagon budget request $1.5T. Heated Hegseth-Khanna exchange: Khanna called $25B figure 'totally off... incompetence.' (4) UAE confirmed OPEC withdrawal effective May 1 — cited Hormuz closure limiting market impact. (5) Trump-Putin phone call: Putin floated temporary Ukraine ceasefire and ideas on Iran's nuclear program. (6) Iran FM Araghchi back in Tehran from Moscow; mediators in Pakistan tell CNN Iran's revised proposal expected by Friday May 1. (7) IDF Chief Zamir on Lebanon border tour: 'no ceasefire' in south Lebanon; Hezbollah drone shot down by IAF over Misgav Am, second target intercepted. (8) CENTCOM Apr 28 disclosed cumulative blockade redirects 39 (+5) and >20 ships moored at Chah Bahar (vs. ~5/day pre-blockade); 31st MEU Marines boarded and released M/V Blue Star III in Arabian Sea Apr 28. (9) Iranian rial hit record low (AP). (10) Pershing Square IPO (PSUS/PS) and Senate Banking advanced Kevin Warsh Fed nomination on party line. AAA national gas avg $4.22/gal Apr 29 (CBS Detroit citing AAA) — highest since August 2022 (vs. $4.18 Apr 28). War Powers May 1 60-day mark T-2 days; H.Con.Res.38 still 94 cosponsors, no floor vote scheduled. Pentagon $25B vs. dashboard's prior ~$51.85B midpoint creates dashboard's largest single discrepancy — reconciliation pending.

Day 62 — BRENT INTRADAY 4-YEAR HIGH $126.41 (ASIA) / TRUMP REJECTS IRAN PROPOSAL / CENTCOM TO BRIEF STRIKE OPTIONS / WAR POWERS T-1: Apr 30 intraday (final closes TBD at compile time, futures pricing -0.2% open): Brent crude June contract spiked to intraday high $126.41/bbl in Asia trading — highest since 2022, four-year wartime high — before paring sharply on Axios report that CENTCOM commander Adm. Brad Cooper would brief President Trump on expanded military options against Iran, reportedly including 'a short and intense wave of strikes.' By 9:53 a.m. ET Brent ~$114.22/bbl (-3.2% vs Apr 29 settlement $118.03); WTI ~$105.38 (-1.4% vs $106.88). June Brent contract expires at session end — July contract becomes front-month. KEY DRIVERS Apr 30: (1) Brent Asia high $126.41 — driven by Trump's Apr 29 Axios rejection of Iran proposal + expectations that blockade extends 'for months'; Trump met with energy executives Apr 29 to discuss minimizing domestic price impact. (2) AAA U.S. retail gasoline national average $4.30/gal — 4-year high since July 2022; +$0.07 d/d, +$0.21 w/w (+5%); +43.8% vs $2.99 Feb 28 baseline. (3) Eurozone April CPI 3.0% (from 2.6% March) — ECB attributes directly to Iran shock, holds at 2%, warns 'longer the war continues, the stronger the impact on broader inflation.' BoE holds 3.75%, raises end-of-year UK forecast above 3.5%. UN ESCAP: ~8.8M Asia-Pacific persons at poverty risk from war. (4) FOMC: Fed held rates steady Apr 29 at Powell's final meeting as chair (term ends May 15); Powell will remain on Board of Governors; cited 'uncertainties surrounding the war in Iran' and warned of continued political attacks on Fed independence. 10Y UST 4.41% (+5 bps post-Fed). (5) Five Mag-7 earnings + Fed decision tail keeping equities pressured; futures -0.2% pre-open. (6) Wheat futures: July contract above $6.595/bu Apr 28 (highest since June 2024); +11% since war started; +6.8% MTD April; +29.8% YTD. Corn +4% MTD April; +6% since war start. (7) BoJ held 0.75% Apr 28; cut 2026 GDP to 0.5% (from 1.0%); raised core CPI to 2.8% (from 1.9%). (8) IDF strikes across southern Lebanon kill at least 9-14 (Lebanese NNA cites 14, Al Jazeera 14, CNN/Reuters 9 initial). (9) Israeli navy intercepts Global Sumud aid flotilla in international waters east of Crete. (10) MAY 1 War Powers Resolution 60-day statutory deadline T-1: H.Con.Res.38 (Massie/Khanna) at 94 cosponsors per congress.gov; latest Senate vote Apr 15 failed 52-47 (4th failure); Time scoop Apr 28: Democrats may sue Trump if he ignores deadline. (11) CENTCOM blockade tally: 42 vessels redirected cumulative (Adm. Cooper Apr 29 HASC testimony, +8 since horizon).

Day 63 — S&P + NASDAQ INTRADAY RECORDS DESPITE DAY 63 OF WAR / BRENT −5% TO $108 ON IRAN PROPOSAL / WPR DEADLINE / ADMIN CLAIMS WAR 'TERMINATED': May 1 intraday (~12:07 pm ET TheStreet/Yahoo Finance live blogs; final closes pending 4 pm ET): S&P 500 ~7,210 (+0.5%) — fresh intraday and closing record on first trading day of May, capping strongest monthly rally since 2020 (April best month for stocks since 2020); Nasdaq Composite ~25,000 (+1.0%); Dow ~49,350 (-0.1% essentially flat); VIX compressed. Brent crude June contract −5.4% intraday to $108.10/bbl (Trading Economics live tracker; Fortune EOD); WTI −4.1% to $101.05/bbl — paring weekly geopolitical premium after Iran's IRNA confirmed Tehran transmitted updated peace proposal to U.S. via Pakistani mediators in response to U.S. amendments to a draft framework. KEY MOVERS: AAPL +~4% on iPhone 17 demand and Q2 earnings beat; Exxon and Chevron beat EPS but reported -45% / -36% net income on Iran-driven oil shock cycle (modest weakness); Spirit Aviation Holdings (FLYVQ, Spirit Airlines parent) reportedly preparing to cease operations after government bailout talks failed (WSJ) — sector-stress signal for U.S. airlines; defense ETF ITA carry-forward (no specific tick observed). KEY DRIVERS May 1: (1) WPR 60-DAY DEADLINE — statutory mark from Trump's Mar 2 notification arrives. Senior administration official (AP) said hostilities 'have terminated' Apr 7/8 for WPR purposes; Brennan Center counsel Katherine Yon Ebright called this 'a sizeable extension of previous legal gamesmanship.' Hegseth (SASC Apr 30): 'we are in a ceasefire right now, which in our understanding means the 60-day clock pauses or stops' — a different theory than 'terminated.' Senate rejected Apr 30 War Powers resolution; Sen. Susan Collins (R-ME) joined Democrats voting yes ('the deadline is not a suggestion; it is a requirement'). Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-AK) said she'll introduce an AUMF when Senate returns from recess week of May 11. Sen. Mike Rounds (R-SD): expects Trump to seek statutory 30-day extension. Congress entered week-long recess; no Democratic lawsuit has been filed as of publication. (2) IRAN PROPOSAL: IRNA confirmed Iran sent latest proposal to Pakistani mediators, who delivered to U.S. Trump separately reaffirmed naval blockade continues 'until [Tehran] cries uncle.' Talks with Mojtaba Khamenei stalled; Khamenei publicly vowed not to relinquish nuclear/missile capabilities and indicated Iran retains Hormuz control. (3) COOPER BRIEF (Apr 30 to Trump): potential limited ground interventions, targeted strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure, other contingencies (SOF News expansion of Axios 'short and intense wave' framing). CENTCOM Apr 29 Dark Eagle hypersonic-weapon AOR request remains pending. (4) CT NATIONAL GUARD: ~150 Soldiers HHC 192nd Military Police Battalion short-notice deployment to ME announced Apr 28 (DVIDS). (5) STATE DEPT 'MARITIME FREEDOM CONSTRUCT' cable Apr 28 to U.S. embassies pitching multilateral coalition to secure Hormuz shipping (WSJ Apr 29). (6) AAA national gas avg $4.392/gal (state-averages page) — but AAA Mid-Atlantic spokesperson Morgan Dean told WVIR/29News May 1 the weekly average 'went down' (likely 7-day-rolling vs same-day methodological mismatch). (7) Equity-oil decoupling extreme: S&P at record + Brent at $108 + Day 63 of war + WPR crisis = textbook 'TACO trade' (markets price an off-ramp regardless of on-ramp evidence). (8) Lebanon ceasefire fragile: Trump (Apr 29 Axios) told Netanyahu strikes must be 'surgical'; mid-May expiry remains operational risk. (9) USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) 'will depart the Middle East in the next few days' after >10-month deployment per SOF News (Apr 30) — UNVERIFIED by Navy; treat as REPORTED INTENT. (10) Iran MoH and Lebanon MoPH cumulative figures unchanged from Apr 30 in available reporting; Lebanese MoPH reconciled to 2,534 killed / 7,863 wounded since Mar 2 (Al Jazeera Apr 29, post-Majdal Zoun). (11) FINAL CLOSES (post-publication update May 2): S&P 500 7,230.12 (+0.29%, NEW RECORD); Nasdaq 25,114.44 (+0.89%, NEW RECORD); Dow 49,499.27 (-0.31%); Russell 2000 2,812.82 (+0.46%); VIX 16.99; Gold $4,644.50; Bitcoin $78,315.88; Apple +3.24% on Q2 earnings beat; XOM +0.81% on Q1 EPS/revenue beat; Spirit (FLYVQ) tumbled on bailout failure; April was best month for S&P 500 and Nasdaq since 2020.
